bullets.text: Full-year companywide CR 87.7; Personal Lines CR 86.8; Commercial Lines CR 87.0; Property CR 107.1 from record hurricane season (30 named storms, 12 U.S. landfalls); NPW grew 8% YOY.
Companywide PIFs grew 11%; personal auto PIF growth 11%; special lines PIF growth 8% as consumers sought outdoor recreation; Robinson (auto+home bundle) customers reached 1.5 million milestone.

Investment portfolio exceeded $47 billion; full-year FTE total return 7.9% (fixed income 6.7%, equity 24.3%); Board declared $4.50/share annual-variable dividend plus $0.10/share quarterly dividends; portfolio grew through volatile credit markets.

Pandemic-driven shelter-in-place orders reduced VMTs and loss frequency significantly in H1; Progressive returned $1.1 billion in credits to personal auto policyholders (Apron Relief Program) plus ~$800M annualized savings via rate reductions in 40+ states.

Personal auto rates reduced ~3% on average in 40+ states representing ~85% of premium from April through December as frequency declined; credits and billing leniency also extended to customers facing financial hardship.

Gallup engagement score reached all-time high — 73% engaged employees vs. 35% U.S. average (98th percentile); Progressive Employee Relief Fund established granting $500–$2,500 to employees facing hardship; employees donated $1M+ of vacation time to coworkers.

Launched Work from Home Superstore campaign produced entirely virtually in employees' homes; Turkey Protection Plan partnership with Whole Foods; total charitable contributions of $21.6M including $4M to Feeding America and $2.5M to Red Cross.

BOP active in 17 states at year-end, added to BusinessQuote Explorer in Q4; SmartHaul surpassed $300M trailing 12-month direct premiums; Snapshot ProView live in 40 states; Lyft coverage expanded to 8 markets; progressive.com/sustainability inaugural report published.

Commercial Lines NPW and PIFs at all-time highs; for-hire transportation and contractor segments rebounded above pre-COVID levels; CL surpassed $5B trailing 12-month NPW milestone; new CL President Karen Bailo named to replace retiring John Barbagallo.

2020 Atlantic hurricane season most active on record; property industry catastrophe losses far above average; COVID created unprecedented regulatory scrutiny over rate adequacy across personal auto insurance industry.
